documentation.value: Defines generic information about the unit that is not dependent on the type of unit. Contains metadata and dependency configuration.
Contains information about the service and the process it supervises. Only applicable for service unit files.
Contains installation information for the unit. Used by `systemctl enable` and `systemctl disable`.
Commands with arguments that are executed when this service is started. Must be an absolute path.
The absolute path to the executable to run
Optional command-line arguments
Commands to execute to stop the service started via ExecStart=.
The absolute path to the executable for stopping the service
Optional command-line arguments
Configures the process start-up type for this service unit.

`simple` (default): process is the main service. `forking`: the process forks and the parent exits. `oneshot`: the process exits after doing its work. `notify`: the service sends a notification when ready.
Configures whether the service shall be restarted when the service process exits, is killed, or a timeout is reached.
`no` (default): service will not be restarted. `on-failure`: restart on non-zero exit code, signal, or timeout. `always`: always restart regardless of exit reason.
Creates a symbolic link in the `.wants/` directory of the listed targets when `systemctl enable` is used.
The target unit(s), e.g. `multi-user.target`, `graphical.target`
Configures ordering dependencies. This unit will be started after the listed units have been started.
One or more unit names to order after, e.g. `network.target`, `syslog.service`
The inverse of After=. This unit will be started before the listed units.
One or more unit names to order before
Sets environment variables for executed processes.
Space-separated list of variable assignments, e.g. `FOO=bar BAZ=qux`
